snippets.title: Counter and defaultdict
snippets.code:
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

words = "the cat the mat the cat".split()
freq = Counter(words)
print(freq.most_common(2))     # [('the', 3), ('cat', 2)]

chunks = [("doc1", "a"), ("doc2", "b"), ("doc1", "c")]
groups = defaultdict(list)
for doc, text in chunks:
    groups[doc].append(text)   # no 'if doc in groups' needed
print(dict(groups))            # {'doc1': ['a', 'c'], 'doc2': ['b']}
snippets.explanation: Counter tallies and ranks; defaultdict(list) auto-creates empty lists on first access.

snippets.title: deque as a sliding window
snippets.code:
from collections import deque

window = deque(maxlen=3)
for turn in ["t1", "t2", "t3", "t4"]:
    window.append(turn)
print(list(window))            # ['t2', 't3', 't4'] - oldest dropped
snippets.explanation: A bounded deque keeps only the most recent N items, ideal for rolling chat context.

snippets.title: A dataclass message
snippets.explanation: The decorator builds __init__/__repr__/__eq__; default_factory gives each instance its own list.

snippets.title: A frozen config value
snippets.code:
from dataclasses import dataclass, replace

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class GenConfig:
    model: str = "gpt-4o"
    temperature: float = 0.7

base = GenConfig()
creative = replace(base, temperature=1.0)   # copy with one change
print(base, creative)
print(hash(base) is not None)               # frozen -> hashable
snippets.explanation: frozen=True makes immutable, hashable config; replace returns a modified copy without mutating the original.
